description_long: This object is a realistic crab with a broad, slightly convex carapace that is mottled reddish-brown with lighter patches. The crab features two prominent claws (chelae) that are robust and slightly asymmetrical, with pointed tips and visible articulation. Its body is supported by eight slender, jointed walking legs that radiate outward, each segment showing a gradient from reddish to pale cream. The dorsal surface of the carapace is textured and slightly rough, with small spines and ridges visible along the edges. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, and short antennae are faintly visible. The underside is paler, and the overall form is compact, typical of a swimming crab found in coastal environments.
description: A reddish-brown crab with a textured carapace, two large claws, and eight slender legs. The eyes are on short stalks, and the body shows natural coloration and detail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, claws, and the forward placement of the eyes and antennae.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the arch of the carapace, the length of the legs, and the profile of the claws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full span of the legs and the symmetrical shape of the carapace.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature of the shell and the articulation of the legs.
